formulas.name: 1. Definition of Inverse Trigonometric Functions
formulas.notes: Inverse trigonometric functions are used to obtain the angle for a given ratio of the sine, cosine, or tangent of that angle.

formulas.name: 2. y = sin^(-1) x
formulas.variables: x = ratio of opposite side to hypotenuse, y = angle in radians
formulas.notes: This formula is used to find the angle whose sine is x.

formulas.name: 3. y = cos^(-1) x
formulas.formula: cos y = x, -1 ≤ x ≤ 1, 0 ≤ y ≤ π
formulas.variables: x = ratio of adjacent side to hypotenuse, y = angle in radians
formulas.notes: This formula is used to find the angle whose cosine is x.

formulas.name: 4. y = tan^(-1) x
formulas.variables: x = ratio of opposite side to adjacent side, y = angle in radians
formulas.notes: This formula is used to find the angle whose tangent is x.

formulas.name: 5. y = cot^(-1) x
formulas.formula: cot y = x, -∞ < x < ∞, 0 < y < π
formulas.variables: x = ratio of adjacent side to opposite side, y = angle in radians
formulas.notes: This formula is used to find the angle whose cotangent is x.

formulas.name: 6. y = sec^(-1) x
formulas.formula: sec y = x, |x| ≥ 1, 0 < y < π/2 or π/2 < y < π
formulas.variables: x = ratio of hypotenuse to adjacent side, y = angle in radians
formulas.notes: This formula is used to find the angle whose secant is x.

formulas.name: 7. y = cosec^(-1) x
formulas.formula: cosec y = x, |x| ≥ 1, -π/2 < y < 0 or 0 < y < π/2
formulas.variables: x = ratio of hypotenuse to opposite side, y = angle in radians
formulas.notes: This formula is used to find the angle whose cosecant is x.
